hypothesis: If a diffusion model framework combining an equivariant graph neural network backbone with an energy-based denoising approach and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement is applied to amorphous material generation, then it will produce structurally valid amorphous configurations with radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, and structure factors that closely match those of reference molecular dynamics simulations, because incorporating physical energy constraints during the reverse diffusion process guides generated structures toward physically realistic local geometries that standard score-matching alone cannot guarantee.
